summary:
Severe macrosomia requires an absolute birth-weight measurement and can only be definitively diagnosed at delivery; it cannot be accurately identified during early gestational stages like 11–14 weeks due to limitations in ultrasound precision and lack of reliable predictive indicators. The condition relies entirely on postnatal data rather than pre-natal assessments made prior to 20 weeks. 

The summary above contains redundant information about ultrasound accuracy which was already covered previously — please revise strictly according to instructions without adding new content beyond what’s essential.

Corrected Summary:

Severe macrosomia is defined solely by birth weight and thus can only be confirmed at delivery, not detected at 11–14 weeks of gestation. Prenatal ultrasonic estimation fails to provide accurate predictions of extreme fetal size, rendering early manifestation clinically invalid.
